cards.headline: Big banks post blockbuster Q2 amid market boom
cards.summary: JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, and Citigroup all reported strong second-quarter results, driven by surging equities trading revenue, the SpaceX IPO, and Iran war volatility. JPMorgan beat profit expectations by the most in five years and raised its outlook for net interest income. Goldman Sachs profits soared, while Bank of America reported a 27% jump in profit and a 15% revenue increase versus a year earlier.

quiz.prompt: What factors were cited as driving big bank revenue in Q2?
options: Rising mortgage rates and home sales
SpaceX IPO, Iran war volatility, and rebound in commercial lending
Federal Reserve rate cuts and bond market rallies
Consumer credit card spending and auto loans
quiz.explanation: Multiple sources cited the SpaceX IPO, Iran war volatility, and a rebound in commercial lending as the key drivers of Wall Street's strong Q2 results.

cards.headline: Oil surges 5% as Trump reinstates Hormuz blockade
cards.summary: Oil prices rose 5% after President Trump reinstated a US blockade of Iranian shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, announcing a 20% toll on the key trade route to cover 'safety and security' costs. The move followed a third consecutive night of US strikes on Iran and prompted gas price volatility for American consumers. Used EV prices also climbed 12% year-on-year as high gas prices drove demand for alternatives.

quiz.prompt: What charge did Trump announce as part of the Hormuz blockade?
options: 20% toll on Iranian shipping through the strait
A 25% tariff on Iranian oil exports
quiz.explanation: Trump announced a 20% charge on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz as part of the reinstated blockade on Iranian shipping.

cards.headline: 12 states sue to block Paramount-Warner Bros merger
cards.summary: A bipartisan group of 12 state attorneys general, led by California, filed a lawsuit to block the $110 billion merger between Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery. Plaintiffs argue the deal would hurt competition and lead to thousands of job losses in Hollywood and TV newsrooms. California joined the suit given that both studios are headquartered in the state.

quiz.prompt: What is the estimated value of the Paramount–Warner Bros. Discovery merger being challenged?
quiz.explanation: The lawsuit describes the Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery merger as a $110 billion deal that attorneys general argue would harm competition.

cards.headline: VW plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs globally
cards.summary: Volkswagen, whose group includes Porsche and Audi, is proposing to slash up to 100,000 jobs and close factories as part of a radical transformation plan. The move follows a steep fall in profits and intensifying competition from Chinese automakers. Workers staged protests at 18 sites across Germany as the proposal was put to the company's board.

quiz.prompt: What has driven Volkswagen to propose cutting up to 100,000 jobs?
options: A government fine over emissions violations
Steep profit declines and tough competition from China
Rising steel and aluminium tariffs from the US
A collapse in European consumer spending
quiz.explanation: BBC and Guardian both report that a steep fall in profits and tough competition from Chinese carmakers are behind VW's drastic restructuring plan.

cards.headline: IBM stock dives after surprise earnings miss
cards.summary: IBM's stock plunged toward its worst single day in nearly 40 years after the company released preliminary earnings that came in well below expectations on both profit and revenue. The surprise miss sent shockwaves through the market, contributing to a broader Dow Jones decline. Multiple sources reported the stock crashing as investors digested the disappointing figures.

quiz.prompt: Why did IBM's stock plunge sharply?
options: It announced a major acquisition at a premium price
It released preliminary earnings well below profit and revenue expectations
It lost a major government contract to a rival
It issued a profit warning tied to the Iran conflict
quiz.explanation: MarketWatch and YahooFin both report that IBM released preliminary earnings that missed expectations on profit and revenue, sending the stock sharply lower.

cards.headline: US refunds $81bn in Trump tariffs after court ruling
cards.summary: The US government has been forced to refund $81 billion in tariffs to companies that imported goods hit by Trump's duties, after the Supreme Court ruled them illegal. The repayments represent a significant legal and financial setback for the administration's trade policy. The development comes as broader tariff policy continues to generate economic and legal uncertainty.

quiz.prompt: Why is the US government refunding $81 billion in tariffs?
options: Congress passed a new trade relief bill
The Supreme Court ruled the tariffs illegal
A WTO arbitration panel ordered repayment
The administration voluntarily reversed the policy
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reports the government was forced to refund $81 billion after the Supreme Court ruled Trump's tariffs illegal.

cards.headline: US home sales fall as prices hit all-time high
cards.summary: Existing home sales dropped in June month over month as mortgage rates remain stubbornly high, according to new data. Despite weaker sales, prices reached an all-time high. A separate CNBC survey found far more real estate agents now report seeing a balanced market, with the share reporting price cuts on active listings dropping dramatically.

quiz.prompt: What happened to US home prices in June despite a drop in sales?
options: They fell to a two-year low
They rose modestly but stayed below prior peaks
quiz.explanation: CNBC reports that even as sales dropped in June due to high mortgage rates, home prices reached an all-time high.

cards.headline: Delta expects higher airfares to persist into 2026
cards.summary: Delta Air Lines reported a $1.4 billion profit in its latest quarter despite recording its highest quarterly fuel expense in history. The airline's CEO said elevated airfares are likely to last, bringing the company's 2026 profit goal within reach. Delta also launched new stripped-down 'basic business' fares without lounge access or seat selection as it restructures its premium cabin offerings.

quiz.prompt: Despite falling oil prices, what did Delta's CEO say about airfares?
options: Fares will fall sharply in the second half of 2025
Elevated airfares are likely to persist
Fares will return to pre-pandemic levels by 2026
Delta plans to offer more discounted routes
quiz.explanation: The Guardian and CNBC both report Delta's CEO said elevated airfares are likely to last despite a recent drop in oil prices.

cards.headline: EasyJet accepts surprise US Apollo takeover bid
cards.summary: EasyJet has agreed to a surprise takeover bid from US firm Apollo, which has trumped a previous offer from rival firm Castlelake. The deal marks a major move by a US private equity player into European aviation. EasyJet described Apollo's bid as the superior offer.

quiz.prompt: Which firm's takeover bid did EasyJet agree to accept?
quiz.explanation: BBC reports that EasyJet agreed to a surprise takeover bid from US firm Apollo, which beat a prior offer from Castlelake.

cards.headline: Toyota invests $3.6bn to move Tacoma production to Texas
cards.summary: Toyota announced it is investing $3.6 billion to shift production of its Tacoma midsize pickup truck from Mexico to its San Antonio, Texas manufacturing campus. The move comes amid ongoing tariff pressures on goods imported from Mexico. It represents one of the largest single manufacturing investments by an automaker in the US in recent years.

quiz.prompt: Where will Toyota move Tacoma pickup truck production from Mexico to?
quiz.explanation: CNBC reports Toyota is investing $3.6 billion to move Tacoma production from Mexico to its San Antonio, Texas campus.

cards.headline: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
cards.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI alleging that a former employee exploited a rare security bug to download confidential files after leaving for the rival company. The suit claims OpenAI's nascent hardware business is built on stolen intellectual property, with allegations ranging from unauthorized system access to job candidates being asked to bring Apple proprietary material. The case marks one of the most high-profile trade-secret clashes between two of tech's biggest players.

quiz.prompt: What did Apple allege a former employee did before joining OpenAI?
options: Exploited a bug to download confidential Apple files
Leaked Apple chip designs to the press
quiz.explanation: Apple's lawsuit claims a former employee exploited a rare bug to download sensitive files from Apple's network after departing for OpenAI.

cards.headline: Meta pulls AI image feature after Instagram privacy outcry
cards.summary: Meta released an AI feature this week allowing users to generate images from public Instagram profile pictures, drawing immediate backlash from privacy advocates who called it a 'recipe for disaster.' The company said users could opt out, but critics argued the default setting put people at risk. Meta subsequently pulled the feature after days of sustained public and regulatory pressure.

quiz.prompt: What was the main privacy concern raised about Meta's new AI image feature?
options: It stored images on foreign servers
It used public Instagram profile pics to generate AI images without prior consent
It shared images with advertisers automatically
It disabled users' ability to delete their accounts
quiz.explanation: Privacy campaigners objected that Meta's feature used public Instagram profile pictures to create AI-generated images, with opt-out rather than opt-in as the default.

cards.headline: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs in major Xbox restructure
cards.summary: Microsoft has announced sweeping layoffs affecting 4,800 employees, equivalent to 2.1% of its total workforce, with 1,600 jobs at Xbox cut immediately. The company described the move as a 'significant restructure,' signalling a major strategic shift in its gaming division. The cuts come as Microsoft continues to invest heavily in AI and cloud infrastructure.

quiz.prompt: What percentage of Microsoft's total workforce do the 4,800 layoffs represent?
quiz.explanation: According to reports, the 4,800 job cuts equate to 2.1% of Microsoft's overall workforce.

cards.headline: Parents warned as AI turns children's photos into abuse material
cards.summary: The UK's National Crime Agency has warned parents about a growing threat of children's images being harvested from social media to create AI-generated child sexual abuse material. Regulators and child safety groups are urging parents to reconsider publicly sharing photos of their children online as nudification and deepfake tools become more sophisticated. A separate class-action lawsuit in the US has expanded against AI companies whose tools were allegedly used to generate explicit images of minors.

quiz.prompt: Which UK agency issued a warning about children's images being misused by AI tools?
quiz.explanation: The National Crime Agency warned that children's images shared publicly online face a growing risk of being used to create AI-generated child abuse material.

cards.headline: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive app features
cards.summary: European regulators have threatened Meta with fines, arguing that features such as infinite scroll on Facebook and Instagram contribute to 'compulsive use' and 'unhealthy habits' among users. The action is part of a broader regulatory push to hold tech platforms accountable for design choices that critics say are engineered to maximise engagement at users' expense. The move adds to Meta's mounting regulatory pressure across Europe.

quiz.prompt: Which specific design feature did EU regulators single out as contributing to compulsive use?
quiz.explanation: EU regulators cited infinite scroll as one of the features they say contributes to compulsive use and unhealthy habits on Facebook and Instagram.

cards.headline: SpaceX cleared for Starship's 13th test flight
cards.summary: SpaceX has received clearance to fly Starship again following a booster failure in May, with the 13th test flight expected later this week. This will be the first Starship test since SpaceX became a publicly traded company, testing investor appetite for the firm's iterative 'fly, fail, fix' development approach. The flight will put Starship under higher pressure and test new Starlink satellite deployment in orbit.

quiz.prompt: What makes SpaceX's upcoming Starship test flight historically notable?
options: It is the first crewed Starship flight
It is the first flight since SpaceX went public
It will attempt a Mars trajectory
It uses a fully reusable heat shield for the first time
quiz.explanation: The upcoming flight is the first Starship test to take place since SpaceX became a publicly traded company.

cards.headline: Big tech carbon emissions surge amid data centre boom
cards.summary: Microsoft, Amazon, and Google have seen their collective carbon emissions rise by nearly a fifth, driven by a construction boom in data centres needed to power AI workloads. The three companies' combined emissions now equal roughly a third of France's total carbon output. Despite the surge, all three firms say they still aim to achieve net-zero emissions.

quiz.prompt: By how much have Microsoft, Amazon, and Google's collective carbon emissions increased?
quiz.explanation: According to reports, the three tech giants' combined carbon emissions have increased by nearly a fifth amid the data centre construction boom.

cards.headline: Hospital replaces nurses with AI, union raises alarm
cards.summary: Twelve utilisation review nurses at Montefiore hospital in New York were laid off and replaced by AI, according to their union, which says the move violated a contract recently won through a strike. The union warned the situation 'should concern every patient who cares about quality of care,' raising broader questions about AI's role in clinical decision-making. The case is being watched as a bellwether for AI displacement in healthcare.

quiz.prompt: Why did Montefiore nurses' union say the AI-driven layoffs were particularly egregious?
options: The nurses were replaced without any notice period
The move violated a contract the union had recently won through a strike
The hospital used an unregulated AI system
The nurses were replaced by overseas contractors
quiz.explanation: The union argued that replacing the nurses with AI violated a contract they had recently secured through strike action.

cards.headline: Waymo called police on teen riders, sparking privacy debate
cards.summary: Waymo disabled a driverless taxi and alerted police after two 15-year-olds were allegedly drinking alcohol and firing toy guns inside the vehicle. The incident has raised questions about the extent of surveillance inside autonomous vehicles and what data companies like Waymo collect and act upon during rides. Privacy advocates say the case highlights the need for clearer policies on passenger monitoring in robotaxis.

quiz.prompt: What action did Waymo take after detecting the teenagers' behaviour in its vehicle?
options: Rerouted them to the nearest police station automatically
Disabled the vehicle and alerted police
Locked the doors until police arrived
Issued an in-app warning and continued the trip
quiz.explanation: Waymo disabled the driverless taxi and contacted police after the two teenagers were allegedly drinking and shooting toy guns inside the vehicle.

cards.headline: Supreme Court allows Texas minors app store law to stand
cards.summary: The US Supreme Court has allowed Texas's App Store Accountability Act to take effect while legal challenges continue in lower courts. The law requires minors to obtain parental permission before downloading most apps, representing one of the most significant state-level moves to regulate children's digital access. Tech industry groups have challenged the legislation, but the Supreme Court declined to block it for now.

quiz.prompt: What does Texas's App Store Accountability Act require of minors?
options: They must use a separate child-only app store
They must have parental permission to download most apps
They are banned from social media until age 16
They must register with the state before creating accounts
quiz.explanation: The Texas law requires minors to have their parents' permission before downloading most apps from app stores.

cards.headline: US strikes Iran as Hormuz blockade resumes fighting
cards.summary: The US military announced a blockade of Iranian ships over the Strait of Hormuz and launched fresh strikes on Iran, with Tehran striking back against Gulf targets and tankers. Trump has threatened to attack a heavily-fortified underground nuclear facility in Iran known as Pickaxe Mountain, while saying a deal remains possible. Senate Minority Leader Schumer publicly criticized Trump over the escalating conflict, and Pakistan is urging dialogue while analysts say it has few tools to halt the latest round of escalation.

quiz.prompt: What strategic waterway is at the center of the latest US-Iran military confrontation?
quiz.explanation: The US announced a blockade of Iranian ships over the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran also vowed to assert control over.

cards.headline: Bangkok bar fire kills 30, locked exits probed
cards.summary: A fire at a popular Bangkok music bar killed at least 30 people and left 24 others in critical condition, making it the city's deadliest blaze in 17 years. Survivors and first responders reported locked doors and a lack of emergency exit signage, and police say negligence is their primary theory. The bar's owner offered 'deepest apologies' as forensic teams investigated the scene, with investigators focusing on the ceiling above the stage area.

quiz.prompt: What did survivors report about the Bangkok bar's emergency exits?
options: They were clearly marked but too narrow
Doors were locked and exit signs were missing
Windows were used as emergency exits
quiz.explanation: Survivors and first responders reported locked doors and a lack of signage marking emergency exits.

cards.headline: ICE agents kill migrants in Maine and Houston
cards.summary: An ICE agent fatally shot a Colombian national during an immigration operation in Maine, coming less than a week after an undocumented migrant was killed by an immigration agent in Houston. Al Jazeera reports that more than 60 people have died in ICE shootings or while in detention since Donald Trump returned to power. Mexico announced it would file criminal complaints over the killing of a Mexican national by ICE agents in Houston, with President Claudia Sheinbaum saying Mexicans were 'outraged.'

quiz.prompt: How did Mexico respond to the killing of a Mexican national by ICE agents in Houston?
options: It recalled its ambassador to Washington
It announced it would file criminal complaints
It imposed trade sanctions on the US
quiz.explanation: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum announced that Mexico would file criminal complaints over the killing of the Mexican national by ICE agents.

cards.headline: Lindsey Graham's sister named to fill Senate seat
cards.summary: South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster appointed Darline Graham, the late senator's younger sister, to serve out the remainder of Lindsey Graham's Senate term after Graham died in office on Saturday at age 71. Top Republicans, including President Trump, coalesced around the pick. The appointment triggers a wide-open primary to determine the GOP nominee for a full Senate term, and Graham's three decades in Congress made him one of the most influential voices on US foreign policy.

quiz.prompt: Who appointed Darline Graham to fill the Senate seat left vacant by Lindsey Graham's death?
quiz.explanation: South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster appointed Darline Graham to serve out the remainder of her late brother's Senate term.

cards.headline: Judge voids Trump's $1.8bn IRS settlement
cards.summary: A US judge voided a $1.8 billion settlement between Donald Trump and the IRS that had granted him immunity from tax audits, ruling the suit was brought for 'improper purposes.' The judge also referred a Trump attorney for possible disciplinary action. The ruling removes a legal shield that had protected Trump from IRS scrutiny under the terms of the voided agreement.

quiz.prompt: Why did a US judge void Trump's $1.8bn IRS settlement?
options: The amount was deemed unconstitutionally large
The suit was brought for improper purposes
The IRS had not approved the terms
Trump's attorneys failed to file on time
quiz.explanation: The judge ruled the suit was brought for 'improper purposes' and referred a Trump attorney for possible disciplinary action.

cards.headline: Houthis fire missiles at Saudi Arabia after Sanaa strikes
cards.summary: Yemen's Iran-backed Houthis launched missiles at Abha airport in Saudi Arabia after blaming Riyadh for strikes on Sanaa's international airport. Analysts warn that if fighting in Yemen expands and the Houthis attempt to block the Bab al-Mandeb strait, the global economy could face a significant new energy shock. The escalation adds another flashpoint to an already volatile regional situation tied to broader US-Iran tensions.

quiz.prompt: Which Saudi airport did the Houthis say they targeted in retaliation for strikes on Sanaa?
quiz.explanation: The Houthis said they targeted Abha airport in Saudi Arabia in response to strikes on Sanaa's airport.

cards.headline: Sudan RSF chief sentenced to death for war crimes
cards.summary: A court convicted Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo, head of Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Forces, in absentia for war crimes, sentencing him to death along with 15 RSF commanders and allies. Separately, the EU announced a ban on gold imports from Sudan to cut off funding for the ongoing war, also restricting exports of mercury and cyanide, chemicals widely used in gold mining. The twin developments mark significant international moves to hold parties accountable for the conflict.

quiz.prompt: Why did the EU ban exports of mercury and cyanide to Sudan alongside its gold import ban?
options: They are used as chemical weapons in the conflict
They are chemicals widely used in gold mining
They are used to manufacture drones
They contaminate water supplies used by civilians
quiz.explanation: The EU restricted exports of mercury and cyanide to Sudan because they are chemicals widely used in gold mining, which finances the war.

cards.headline: Wildfire near Paris may have been deliberately set
cards.summary: A wildfire raging south of Paris has scorched around 1,300 hectares, with France's interior minister saying the blaze could have been set deliberately and two people have been arrested. France has deployed 70,000 security personnel across the country for Bastille Day and World Cup semifinal events. The fire is striking as France endures its third heatwave in three months, with unusually warm rivers already affecting nuclear power plant cooling.

quiz.prompt: What has France's interior minister said about the cause of the wildfire near Paris?
options: It was caused by a lightning strike
It started from an unattended campfire
It could have been set deliberately, with two arrested
It was sparked by downed power lines
quiz.explanation: France's interior minister said the fire near Paris could have been set deliberately, and two people have been arrested in connection with it.

cards.headline: Trump grants Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles
cards.summary: President Trump has granted Ukraine permission to produce Patriot air-defense missiles domestically, a significant shift that could bolster the country's long-term defense capacity. Defense analysts note the move could reduce Ukraine's dependence on US deliveries and strengthen its ability to sustain air defenses. The development comes as Ukraine continues to face ongoing military pressure.

quiz.prompt: What has President Trump granted Ukraine permission to do regarding Patriot missiles?
options: Purchase them at a discounted rate
quiz.explanation: Trump granted Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles domestically, which analysts say could strengthen the country's long-term air defense capacity.

cards.headline: Hungary parliament removes president from office
cards.summary: The Hungarian parliament voted to remove President Tamás Sulyok from office, a figure widely seen as a loyalist of former prime minister Viktor Orbán, who lost power in April after 16 years in government. The removal marks another shift in Hungary's political landscape following Orbán's departure from power. No further details on the grounds for removal were provided in the reports.

quiz.prompt: How long had Viktor Orbán held power in Hungary before losing it in April?
quiz.explanation: Viktor Orbán lost power in April after 16 years as prime minister, and the removed president Tamás Sulyok was widely seen as his loyalist.

cards.headline: England face Argentina in high-security Atlanta semi-final
cards.summary: England will play Argentina in a World Cup semi-final in Atlanta on Wednesday, a fixture steeped in historical rivalry. Declan Rice is fit and available to start after overcoming illness. Atlanta police have increased staffing and resources for the match, with England fans' behaviour described as 'exemplary' so far in the tournament.

quiz.prompt: Where is the England vs Argentina World Cup semi-final being held?
quiz.explanation: The snippets confirm Atlanta is hosting the England vs Argentina World Cup semi-final, with Atlanta police stepping up security for the match.

cards.headline: France face Spain in 'spectacular' Dallas semi-final
cards.summary: France and Spain meet in a World Cup semi-final in Dallas, with France midfielder Warren Zaïre-Emery saying his team want revenge on Spain after a previous Euros defeat. France boss Didier Deschamps is predicting a 'spectacular' game. The match also sets up a high-profile duel between Kylian Mbappé and teenage Spanish star Lamine Yamal.

quiz.prompt: What did France midfielder Zaïre-Emery say France want against Spain?
options: To win their third consecutive trophy
To break the World Cup scoring record
To prove Mbappé is world's best
quiz.explanation: According to the snippets, Zaïre-Emery said France want to 'exact revenge on Spain and banish' memories of a previous Euros result.

cards.headline: Lamine Yamal turns 19 ahead of France semi-final
cards.summary: Spain's Lamine Yamal celebrated his 19th birthday ahead of the World Cup semi-final against France, saying 'what I want as a present is a win.' The teenage winger is set for a marquee individual duel with France's Kylian Mbappé. Yamal's three-year-old brother Keyne has also become a viral sensation among fans at the tournament.

quiz.prompt: What did Lamine Yamal say he wanted as a birthday present?
quiz.explanation: The snippets quote Yamal saying 'What I want as a present is a win' ahead of the semi-final against France.

cards.headline: Harry Kane enters defining World Cup week for career
cards.summary: England striker Harry Kane is described as entering the most defining week of his career, with a World Cup final and Ballon d'Or potentially within reach. Outshining Lionel Messi against Argentina is cited as a key route to cementing his legacy as an all-time great beyond England. Kane is driven by what is described as a sense of destiny.

quiz.prompt: What individual award is cited as potentially within reach for Harry Kane this week?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian snippet states the Ballon d'Or is within reach for Kane, with outshining Messi identified as the key route to winning it.

cards.headline: Norway welcome 100,000 fans despite semi-final exit
cards.summary: More than 100,000 fans flooded the streets of Oslo to celebrate Norway's World Cup campaign despite their semi-final exit. The squad performed one last 'Viking row' on the palace steps during the parade. The celebrations briefly had to be paused due to low-hanging overhead cables.

quiz.prompt: What did the Norway squad perform on the palace steps during their homecoming parade?
quiz.explanation: According to the Guardian, the Norway squad performed one last 'Viking row' on the palace steps during their homecoming parade in Oslo.

cards.headline: Man City eye Morocco teenager Bouaddi for £85m
cards.summary: Manchester City are intent on signing Lille's 18-year-old Moroccan midfielder Ayyoub Bouaddi for around £85m. Bouaddi impressed during the World Cup and the deal would take City's spending on two midfielders to around £200m. The story is corroborated by both the Guardian and Sky Sports.

quiz.prompt: How much are Manchester City reportedly willing to pay for Ayyoub Bouaddi?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reports Manchester City are intent on signing Bouaddi for €100m, equivalent to £85m.

cards.headline: Jordan Walker wins 2026 Home Run Derby in Philadelphia
cards.summary: Jordan Walker won the 2026 MLB Home Run Derby at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia, denying hometown favourite Kyle Schwarber and the hostile Phillies fans a title. The All-Star break has taken over Philadelphia, with the Home Run Derby and All-Star Game both held there. Tigers rookie Kevin McGonigle, 21, also starred at the All-Star Game in the same stadium where he used to be a fan.

quiz.prompt: Who did Jordan Walker defeat to win the 2026 Home Run Derby?
quiz.explanation: CBSSports reports Walker won the 2026 Home Run Derby by denying Kyle Schwarber and the Phillies fans a home title at Citizens Bank Park.

cards.headline: Brighton sign Vuskovic from Spurs for record £46m
cards.summary: Brighton have signed Croatia defender Luka Vuskovic from Tottenham Hotspur for a club-record fee of £46m on a five-year contract, with an option for a further year. The deal is confirmed by both BBC and Sky Sports. It represents Brighton's biggest ever transfer outlay.

quiz.prompt: How much did Brighton pay for Luka Vuskovic?
quiz.explanation: Both BBC and Sky Sports confirm Brighton signed Vuskovic from Spurs for a club-record fee of £46m.

cards.headline: McGregor fights again after comeback lasts 69 seconds
cards.summary: Conor McGregor's return to UFC in Las Vegas ended in the first round after just 69 seconds. Despite the swift defeat, McGregor says he plans to fight again in the UFC and will undergo surgery on his leg. The brief comeback was widely anticipated after a lengthy absence from competition.

quiz.prompt: How long did Conor McGregor's UFC comeback bout last?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reports McGregor's return to UFC in Las Vegas finished in the first round after just 69 seconds.

cards.headline: India beat England by 270 runs in historic Lord's Test
cards.summary: India bowled out England before lunch to seal an emphatic 270-run victory in the historic women's Test match at Lord's. India took the required four wickets in the final session to complete a dominant win. The result represents a significant moment in women's Test cricket at the home of the game.

quiz.prompt: By how many runs did India beat England in the women's Test at Lord's?
quiz.explanation: BBC reports India sealed a 270-run victory over England in the historic women's Test match at Lord's.
